title: A Portrait.
content:
A face devoid of love or grace,
    A hateful, hard, successful face,
    A face with which a stone
    Would feel as thoroughly at ease
    As were they old acquaintances, --
    First time together thrown.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "A Portrait.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
